title: Device Drivers
summary: Explains what device drivers are, how they fit in the OS architecture, and why the modular driver structure allows hardware changes without reinstalling the OS.
content_points: Drivers manage communication with specific peripheral devices; run in kernel mode.
Not part of the kernel but controlled by it; called from interrupt handling routines.
Modular: replace a device → replace its driver; no OS reinstall needed.
learning_objectives: Describe the role of device drivers
Explain how modular driver design improves maintainability
